pattern: Convexity makes both goals agree: concentrating the digit sum into the leftmost positions maximizes the squared total and yields the largest number simultaneously.
hints: Squaring is convex, so a fixed digit sum produces a larger square total when concentrated into fewer, bigger digits rather than spread out.
Concentrating value into the most significant positions also makes the number itself larger, so one greedy serves both objectives.
Sweep left to right placing the biggest digit each spot can hold (min of 9 and the remaining sum); the answer exists only when the sum fits in num nines.

edge_inputs.description: one over all nines is impossible
leading digit must stay at least one

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    num = max(1, min(size, 2000))
    s = rng.randrange(1, 9 * num + 1)
    return [num, s]
stress.note: sum kept within 9*num so a valid number exists

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    num = max(1, min(size, 100))
    s = rng.randrange(1, 9 * num + 30)
    return [num, s]
stress.note: may exceed 9*num to exercise the empty-string case
